title: Azure DevOps Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Azure DevOps Services** is Microsoft's hosted DevOps platform exposing a uniform REST API under `https://dev.azure.com/{organization}` (with sister hosts `https://vssps.dev.azure.com/{organization}` for Graph users and `https://vsrm.dev.azure.com/{organization}` for classic Release Management). This connector — `AzureDevopsConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "azure_devops"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from an Azure DevOps organization:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Projects | `/_apis/projects` | List + read projects (stateFilter, continuation token) |
| Teams | `/_apis/projects/{project}/teams` | List teams under a project |
| Users | `https://vssps.dev.azure.com/{org}/_apis/graph/users` | Graph user enumeration |
| Repos | `/{project}/_apis/git/repositories` | List + read Git repositories |
| Pull Requests | `/{project}/_apis/git/repositories/{repo}/pullrequests` | List, get, create |
| Work Items (WIQL) | `/{project}/_apis/wit/wiql` + `/_apis/wit/workitems` | Query + batch-fetch + CRUD via JSON-patch |
| Builds | `/{project}/_apis/build/builds` | List, get, queue |
| Pipelines | `/{project}/_apis/pipelines` | List YAML pipelines |
| Releases | `https://vsrm.dev.azure.com/{org}/{project}/_apis/release/releases` | List classic-RM releases |

The connector normalises work items into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), exposes every operation as a standalone public `async def` on `AzureDevopsConnector` (OCP), retries 429/5xx with `Retry-After`-aware exponential backoff (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::AzureDevOpsHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Provision a Personal Access Token

1. Sign in to https://dev.azure.com.
2. Open **User Settings → Personal Access Tokens**.
3. Click **New Token**, scope it to the surfaces you intend to use (Code: Read & Write, Work Items: Read & Write, Build: Read & Execute, Release: Read & Write).
4. Copy the token value — Azure DevOps shows it only once.
5. Note your **organization slug** (the `{org}` segment of `https://dev.azure.com/{org}`).

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Azure DevOps**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `organization` (text) | Your organization slug, e.g. `contoso` |
| `pat` (secret) | The PAT from step 4 |
| `api_version` (text, optional) | Defaults to `7.1` |
| `default_project` (text, optional) | Project used by `sync()` and methods where `project` is omitted |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 200 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `organization` + `pat` are present (it does NOT call the API), initialises `AzureDevOpsHTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, AUTHENTICATED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which probes `GET /_apis/projects?$top=1&api-version=7.1`. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `DEGRADED + TOKEN_EXPIRED` (PAT rejected — re-issue it). On 429 → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Azure DevOps REST API uses **Personal Access Token (PAT) authentication** wired through HTTP Basic (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`).

### Credential model

- The PAT is sent in the `Authorization` header as `Basic base64(":<pat>")` — **empty username, PAT as password**. This is the Azure DevOps convention; sending the PAT as a Bearer token will return 401.
- The HTTP client transparently passes through pre-formed `Bearer <token>` strings, so a future OAuth migration only needs to swap the credential resolver.
- Every request URL carries `?api-version={api_version}` (default `7.1`). Omitting the param returns 400.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `organization` + `pat`. Initialises `AzureDevOpsHTTPClient`. Does **not** call any Azure DevOps endpoint. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a `TokenInfo` whose `access_token` is the configured PAT (for surface compatibility with the BaseConnector ABI). |
| `health_check()` | `GET /_apis/projects?$top=1` — minimal probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — PATs do not refresh. |
| token storage | The PAT is persisted via `save_config()` in `install()`; no separate token store. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
Authorization: Basic base64(":<pat>")
Accept:        application/json;api-version=7.1
Content-Type:  application/json                ← or application/json-patch+json for work-item CRUD
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `organization` | text | yes | `self.organization` | `{organization}` segment of the base URL |
| `pat` | secret | yes | `self.pat` | PAT used to build the HTTP Basic header |
| `personal_access_token` | secret | — | `self.pat` (alias) | Legacy alias accepted by `install()` for back-compat |
| `api_version` | text | no | `self.api_version` (default `7.1`) | `?api-version=` query param on every request |
| `default_project` | text | no | `self.default_project` | Default project for `sync()` |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 200) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["organization", "pat"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_DEFAULT_API_VERSION = "7.1"
_DEFAULT_RATE_LIMIT = 200
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `AzureDevopsConnector`. SOC: `connector.py` orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::AzureDevOpsHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `organization` + `pat`; initialise `AzureDevOpsHTTPClient`. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No OAuth exchange — surfaces the configured PAT as `TokenInfo(token_type="Basic")`.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Probes `GET /_apis/projects?$top=1&api-version=7.1`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Runs a WIQL query over `default_project` (`SELECT [System.Id] FROM WorkItems`), batches detail fetches in groups of 200, normalizes each work item via `normalize_work_item`, and ingests through `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult(documents_found / _synced / _failed)`.

children.title: Projects · Teams · Users
children.content:
### `async list_projects(*, state_filter="wellFormed", top=100, continuation_token=None) -> Dict`
`GET /_apis/projects` — supports stateFilter + `$top` + continuationToken.

### `async get_project(project_id_or_name) -> Dict`
`GET /_apis/projects/{idOrName}`.

### `async list_teams(project, *, top=100, skip=0) -> Dict`
`GET /_apis/projects/{project}/teams`.

### `async list_users(*, top=100, continuation_token=None) -> Dict`
`GET https://vssps.dev.azure.com/{org}/_apis/graph/users` (Graph host).

children.title: Repos + Pull Requests
children.content:
### `async list_repos(project) -> Dict`
`GET /{project}/_apis/git/repositories`.

### `async get_repo(project, repository_id) -> Dict`
`GET /{project}/_apis/git/repositories/{id}`.

### `async list_pull_requests(project, repository_id, *, status="active", top=100) -> Dict`
`GET /{project}/_apis/git/repositories/{repo}/pullrequests?searchCriteria.status={status}`.

### `async get_pull_request(project, repository_id, pull_request_id) -> Dict`
`GET /{project}/_apis/git/repositories/{repo}/pullrequests/{id}`.

### `async create_pull_request(project, repository_id, title, source_ref, target_ref, description="", reviewers=None) -> Dict`
`POST /{project}/_apis/git/repositories/{repo}/pullrequests` — body uses camelCase: `{sourceRefName, targetRefName, title, description, reviewers?}`.

children.title: Work Items (WIQL)
children.content:
### `async query_work_items(project, wiql) -> Dict`
`POST /{project}/_apis/wit/wiql` — body `{query: "<WIQL>"}`. Returns refs only.

### `async list_work_items(project, wiql) -> Dict`
Runs the WIQL query then batches detail fetches via `GET /_apis/wit/workitems?ids=...` (200 at a time). Returns `{workItems: [...refs...], value: [...full items...]}`.

### `async get_work_item(work_item_id, fields=None) -> Dict`
`GET /_apis/wit/workitems/{id}` with optional `fields=` projection.

### `async create_work_item(project, work_item_type, fields) -> Dict`
`POST /{project}/_apis/wit/workitems/${type}` — body is JSON-patch (`[{op:"add", path:"/fields/<key>", value}, ...]`) with `Content-Type: application/json-patch+json`.

### `async update_work_item(work_item_id, fields) -> Dict`
`PATCH /_apis/wit/workitems/{id}` — same JSON-patch shape as create.

children.title: Builds · Pipelines · Releases
children.content:
### `async list_builds(project, *, status_filter=None, top=50) -> Dict`
`GET /{project}/_apis/build/builds?statusFilter={...}&$top={N}`.

### `async get_build(project, build_id) -> Dict`
`GET /{project}/_apis/build/builds/{id}`.

### `async queue_build(project, definition_id, *, source_branch=None, parameters=None) -> Dict`
`POST /{project}/_apis/build/builds` — body `{definition:{id}, sourceBranch?, parameters?}`. `parameters` is JSON-encoded into a string per Azure DevOps' wire convention.

### `async list_pipelines(project, *, top=100, continuation_token=None) -> Dict`
`GET /{project}/_apis/pipelines`.

### `async list_releases(project, *, definition_id=None, top=50) -> Dict`
`GET https://vsrm.dev.azure.com/{org}/{project}/_apis/release/releases` (classic RM host).

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
AzureDevOpsError                          # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── AzureDevOpsAuthError                  # 401 / 403
├── AzureDevOpsBadRequestError            # 400
├── AzureDevOpsNotFoundError              # 404
├── AzureDevOpsConflictError              # 409 (revision conflict)
├── AzureDevOpsRateLimitError             # 429 — retry_after_s
└── AzureDevOpsServerError                # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
AzureDevOpsNotFound     = AzureDevOpsNotFoundError
AzureDevOpsNetworkError = AzureDevOpsServerError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 | Raise immediately (non-transient) |
| 429 | Honour `Retry-After` header; otherwise exponential backoff (0.5s, 1s, 2s, capped at 8s) — up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3` |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff — up to 3 retries |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Backoff + retry up to 3, then raise `AzureDevOpsNetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps them via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  TOKEN_EXPIRED)        ← PAT rejected, re-issue token
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)  ← PAT missing required scopes
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)            ← logged, traffic still flowing
5xx → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)            ← retried at HTTP layer
network → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `organization` or `pat` blank | Re-enter both in ACP. Azure DevOps shows the PAT only once at generation — rotate if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 from `/_apis/projects` — PAT expired, revoked, or scoped to a different tenant | Issue a new PAT under **User Settings → Personal Access Tokens**, paste into the install field. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 — PAT lacks the scope needed by the probe (or by a method you called) | Edit the PAT and add the missing scope (Code: Read for repos/PRs, Work Items: Read for WIQL, Build: Read for builds, Release: Read for releases). |
| `azure_devops.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (TSTU-based throttle) | The client honours `Retry-After` and retries. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer concurrent requests. |
| `400` on a work-item CRUD call | Wrong Content-Type or malformed JSON-patch | `create_work_item` / `update_work_item` automatically set `Content-Type: application/json-patch+json` and build the patch array. Confirm you are calling the connector methods, not the raw HTTP client. |
| `404` on a project-scoped call | Misspelled project name or PAT scoped to a different organization | Use `list_projects()` to confirm the exact `name` (case-sensitive). Verify `organization` matches the slug that issued the PAT. |
| Releases call returns 404 | `vsrm.dev.azure.com` not enabled for the project (classic Release Management disabled) | Confirm classic RM is enabled in the Azure DevOps project settings, or use the YAML-pipelines-based release model instead. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'AzureDevOpsNetworkError'` | Older `exceptions.py` without the back-compat alias | Already fixed — `AzureDevOpsNetworkError = AzureDevOpsServerError` alias added in `exceptions.py`. |
